fragility_points: ETH could move materially away from 2400 before printing a qualifying high.
The market may be overpaying for nearness and underpricing failure-to-tag risk.
key_assumptions: ETH remains close enough to 2400 for ordinary intraday volatility to produce a brief qualifying high.
The main thing the market is pricing is threshold proximity plus permissive contract mechanics, not a major fresh catalyst.
main_logical_chain: The market's source of truth is a permissive Binance 1-minute high rule rather than a sustained close.
ETH is already trading just below the threshold on independent live price checks.
Therefore a high Yes probability is rational, though the current price still looks a bit rich rather than perfectly efficient.
main_thesis: The high Yes price is mostly justified because the contract resolves on any Binance 1-minute high at or above 2400 and ETH is already trading just below that threshold.
quote_anchors: price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title
source_quality_view: Primary rule source quality is high and source-of-truth ambiguity is low; live price verification quality is good but evidence independence is only medium.
strongest_disconfirmers: At >90% implied probability, even ordinary downside path risk or repeated rejection below 2400 matters.
A modest crypto selloff or volatility slump could keep Binance highs below the threshold through the remaining window.
strongest_supports: Polymarket rule resolves Yes on any Binance 1-minute high >= 2400 during Apr 13-19.
Binance and CoinGecko spot checks both placed ETH around 2388 during review, only about 0.5% below the trigger.
Several days remain in a 24/7 market, which favors touch-event probability.
timing_relevance: High, because this is a date-bounded weekly touch market and multiple days remain.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much of the current price reflects informed flow versus simple threshold-chasing is still unclear.
what_would_change_view: A material move away from 2400, broader crypto risk-off pressure, anomalously weak Binance highs, or an unsupported further rise toward certainty would make me more bearish on Yes.
